alert_summary: Repeated SSH authentication failures from two sources against privileged and user accounts; automated brute-force pattern with 400+ failures in 14 minutes.

reasons.challenge_id: challenge_id matches attack_type for correlation with playbooks.
reasons.severity: High: high failure volume, privileged account (root) in target set, and sustained parallel sources increase compromise risk.
reasons.alert_summary: Distilled from rule firings, IP list, user list, and time window in the aggregated incidents.
reasons.remediation_proposal: Block both attacker IPs at the edge firewall; enforce MFA on SSH for privileged accounts; disable password auth for root if policy allows; review successful SSH sessions after the window for persistence.

detection.attack_type: SSH failure bursts and rule thresholds indicate brute force, not HTTP/SQL patterns.
attacker_ips: Dominant source by failure volume in the aggregation window.
Secondary source with overlapping timeline and same victim set.
victim_accounts: Most targeted privileged account in the window.
Secondary account with repeated failures from the same sources.
detection.attack_start_time: Earliest significant SSH failure after noise exclusion.
detection.attack_end_time: Last peak before activity drops to baseline.
detection.indicators: rule_ids from fired rules; failure_count and distinct_usernames derived from aggregated incidents.

reasons.detection_time_seconds: Pipeline constant (300) from prompt, not inferred from raw logs.

exhaustive_analysis: Between 08:00 and 08:14 UTC, two sources sent repeated SSH authentication failures against root and ubuntu. Rules ssh_burst and ssh_failures exceeded thresholds; over 400 failures and two distinct usernames targeted. Timeline and IP overlap suggest automated brute force. Recommend blocking sources, reviewing password policy, and checking for successful logins after the window.
remediation_proposal: 1) Block 198.51.100.10 and 198.51.100.11 at perimeter and SSH tier. 2) Rotate credentials for root/ubuntu if any success occurred; enable MFA or key-only auth. 3) Review sshd_config (PermitRootLogin, MaxAuthTries). 4) Hunt for lateral movement from same subnets in the following 24h.
